note: One post per date. Buffer agent posts the entry matching today's date. Reddit posts spread every 2-3 days. Medium posts weekly.

posts.text:
168,000 parcels indexed across the Coachella Valley.

Every permit filed. Every ownership transfer. Every valuation shift — mapped and searchable in real time.

This is the data that tells you which neighborhoods are quietly accelerating before anyone else notices. Which properties are priced against missing permit information. Which sellers are sitting on equity they do not know they have.

This is PropertyDNA.

www.thepropertydna.com/market-heatmaps

Unpopular opinion: the CMA is dead.

Not because the data is wrong. Because the prep takes 3 hours for a 30-minute conversation.

We generate a full property intelligence report — comps, permits, valuation, hazard, rental demand — in under 60 seconds. Same data. Zero prep time.

The agents winning listing appointments in 2026 are sending the report to the seller 24 hours early. They walk into a conversation instead of a cold pitch.

www.thepropertydna.com

The most important number in real estate right now: 21.

That's the median days on market in Palm Springs. It was 42 days this time last year.

When DOM cuts in half, everything changes. Buyers need to decide faster. Offers need to be cleaner. Agents who walk in prepared — with data — close more.

The PropertyDNA heat map tracks DOM by zip in real time.

www.thepropertydna.com/market-heatmaps

We found an unpermitted addition on a listing before our buyer made an offer.

The addition was 300 sqft. The seller was advertising it in the total square footage. That's a financing problem, an appraisal problem, and a disclosure problem.

Permit check saved our client from a deal that would have blown up at inspection.

We now run permit history on every property, automatically, before our clients even schedule a showing.

www.thepropertydna.com

What 168,000 parcels of Coachella Valley data tells us:

• Movie Colony: ppsf up 8.3% YoY — inventory below 2019 levels
• South Palm Springs: 90-day inventory building — first time since 2022
• Cathedral City Cove: investor activity rising — 23% of Q1 sales to LLCs
• Palm Desert Country Club: permit pull rate up 31% — signal of incoming appreciation

The market isn't uniform. It's 12 different markets inside one county.

www.thepropertydna.com/market-heatmaps

The listing appointment script that's working for us right now:

Step 1: Send PropertyDNA report to seller 24 hours before meeting.
Step 2: Walk in and ask "what questions did the report bring up?"
Step 3: Answer their questions with your expertise.
Step 4: Price the listing.

The old script: show up, present comps, overcome objections about price, fight for the listing.

The new script: the research arrived before you did. You're already the expert.

www.thepropertydna.com/blog/win-listing-appointment-ai-property-data

Real estate investors: absentee owner + 10+ years of ownership + zero permits pulled in the last 5 years = motivated seller candidate.

This isn't theory. We filtered 168,000 Coachella Valley parcels with this logic and identified 2,400 properties matching the profile.

First 300 outreach letters: 12 responses, 2 deals under contract.

The best off-market leads are hiding in public records. You just need the right filter.

www.thepropertydna.com/blog/off-market-property-leads

Short-term rental data that Palm Springs agents need to know:

Old Las Palmas / Movie Colony: ADR $420, 64% occupancy — architecture commands a 47% premium over central PS
Central Palm Springs: ADR $285, 71% occupancy — highest occupancy, driven by walkability
Cathedral City: ADR $195, 58% occupancy — price-sensitive market, Coachella spike dependent

If your buyer is STR-motivated, neighborhood selection is everything. Central PS = occupancy. Old Las Palmas = nightly rate.

www.thepropertydna.com

Why I stopped using Zillow's Zestimate for client conversations.

It has a 2.4% median error on listed homes. Fine.

But on off-market homes? 6.9% error. On a $900,000 property that's a $62,100 swing — in either direction.

The model doesn't see permits. A home with a permitted casita addition might be worth $75,000 more than its Zestimate. A home with 300sqft of unpermitted additions might appraise for less.

We stopped citing Zestimates. We send the PropertyDNA report instead.

www.thepropertydna.com/blog/zillow-zestimate-accuracy

Permit velocity is the leading indicator most agents don't track.

When a neighborhood sees a surge in remodel permits — kitchens, ADUs, pool additions — appreciation follows 12-18 months later. The money moves in before the prices do.

We track permit pull rates across every zip in the Coachella Valley. Right now:
• 92262 (PS Central): permits up 14% YoY
• 92260 (Palm Desert): ADU permits up 41% — largest jump in the valley
• 92253 (La Quinta): renovation permits down 8% — early inventory signal

www.thepropertydna.com/market-heatmaps

An agent on our team ran the numbers on her listing appointments from the last 6 months.

• Appointments where she sent a PropertyDNA report in advance: 71% conversion to signed listing
• Appointments without the report: 44% conversion

Same agent. Same market. Same price range.

The report isn't closing the deal. It's changing the room she walks into. Informed sellers sign faster.

www.thepropertydna.com

The 5 questions to ask before buying in any HOA — and why most agents skip them:

1. What % is the reserve fund? (Under 70% = special assessment risk)
2. Any pending litigation? (Ask for last 2 years of board minutes)
3. What's the rental restriction policy? (Affects future buyer pool)
4. Have dues increased more than once in 5 years?
5. What's the deferred maintenance list?

We include HOA risk signals in every PropertyDNA report.

The answers are in the documents. Most people never ask for them.

www.thepropertydna.com/blog/hoa-questions-before-buying

Hot take: the 3-hour CMA is a liability, not a value-add.

Here's why: you spend 3 hours pulling comps, building a presentation, and pricing a home — before you've even met the seller or seen the property.

All that prep is based on assumptions. The property might have issues you don't know about. The seller might have a completely different price expectation.

We generate the data report in 60 seconds. We spend the 3 hours on relationship — understanding the seller's situation, their timeline, what matters to them.

Data fast. Empathy slow.

www.thepropertydna.com

The Coachella Valley market by the numbers — May 2026:

📊 Palm Springs: Median DOM 21 days | Supply 1.8 months | YoY appreciation +4.2%
📊 Palm Desert: Median DOM 28 days | Supply 2.1 months | YoY appreciation +3.1%
📊 Rancho Mirage: Median DOM 19 days | Supply 1.4 months | YoY appreciation +5.7%
📊 Indian Wells: Median DOM 34 days | Supply 2.8 months | YoY appreciation +2.9%
📊 La Quinta: Median DOM 24 days | Supply 2.0 months | YoY appreciation +3.8%

Full heat map: www.thepropertydna.com/market-heatmaps

Something we didn't expect when we started sending AI property reports to buyers:

They started asking better questions.

Instead of "is this a good deal?" they were asking "why is the flood zone rating different from the neighbor's?" and "what does the permit history tell us about how the owners maintained the property?"

Better questions lead to better decisions. Better decisions lead to fewer post-close surprises.

The report doesn't replace agent expertise. It upgrades the conversation.

www.thepropertydna.com

The hidden premium in Palm Springs real estate: mid-century modern architecture.

We analyzed 340 sales in the last 12 months. Verified MCM homes (built 1940-1975, original architectural integrity) sold at a 22% premium over comparable non-MCM properties — adjusting for size, condition, and location.

The premium is highest in Movie Colony (28%) and lowest in Desert Highland (11%).

Buyers paying the MCM premium are buying a finite inventory. There are no new ones being built.

www.thepropertydna.com/market-heatmaps

What Rancho Mirage's 1.4 months of supply actually means for buyers:

At 6 months of supply, buyers have leverage. At 3 months, it's balanced. At 1.4 months, sellers control everything.

In Rancho Mirage right now:
• Average offer-to-list ratio: 101.3%
• Median days to offer: 8
• % of listings with multiple offers: 67%

If you're putting a buyer into Rancho Mirage without a pre-offer property report, you're putting them in a gunfight without ammunition.

www.thepropertydna.com/market-heatmaps

Memorial Day weekend real estate reality check:

Palm Springs sees its biggest buyer traffic surge of the year in late May. Seasonal buyers from LA and Phoenix visit for the long weekend and buy.

This year they're walking into a market with 1.8 months of supply and a median DOM of 21 days.

If you're representing a buyer this weekend: have the property report ready before the showing. Offers are being written the same day.

www.thepropertydna.com

This $30M Palm Springs estate has a 70-year secret most agents miss.

It's not the architect (verified Albert Frey). It's not the celebrity owner. It's the fact that the original drawings sit in the UCSB archive — and the current listing photo doesn't reference any of it.

We pulled the dossier. https://www.thepropertydna.com/dossier/513110020

Comment DOSSIER and we'll DM you the full provenance file.

#palmsprings #midcenturymodern #albertfrey #realestate #luxuryrealestate #palmspringsstyle #architecture #proptech

70% of "celebrity owned" home claims aren't documentable. We verify the other 30%.

Frank Sinatra's Twin Palms (1148 E Alejo). Elvis's Honeymoon Hideaway (1350 Ladera Circle). Bob Hope's Lautner (2466 Southridge). Each has a deed record. Each has a magazine feature. Each has a verified provenance score.

We documented 53 of them.

Comment VERIFIED to get the full list.

#palmsprings #celebrityhomes #realestate #midcenturymodern #provenance #luxuryrealestate #propertyhistory

We mapped every Palm Springs home Frank Sinatra ever owned. Here are 4.

1947–1957: Twin Palms (E. Stewart Williams, piano-shaped pool)
1957–1995: Sinatra Compound, Rancho Mirage (JFK 1962 visit documented)
1960s: Movie Colony rental
1970s: Tamarisk Country Club holdings

Every owner. Every architect. Every press feature. Verified.

DM "SINATRA" and I'll send you the full provenance map.

#franksinatra #palmsprings #celebrityhomes #ratpack #midcenturymodern #realestate

I tour Palm Springs homes for a living. This one shocked me.

Albert Frey designed his own house in 1963 by literally building it around a boulder. Today, you can still see the 8-foot granite slab inside the living room.

It's the Frey House II. 686 Palisades Drive. Only ~3 owners in 60 years.

Comment FREY for the architect's full Palm Springs portfolio.

#albertfrey #palmsprings #midcenturymodern #architecture #freyhouse #desertmodernism

James Bond fought a fight scene here. It's a real Palm Springs home you can see.

The Elrod House. 2175 Southridge. Designed by John Lautner in 1968. Featured in Diamonds Are Forever (1971) — the scene with Bambi and Thumper.

It's still privately owned. Provenance verified through the Lautner Foundation archive.

Comment BOND for the dossier with the film clips.

#jamesbond #johnlautner #palmsprings #elrodhouse #midcenturymodern #architecture #moviemagic

Could you live in Bob Hope's house?

$30M+ Lautner-designed home in Indian Canyons. Hope commissioned it in 1973; rebuilt after a fire. The mushroom-shaped roof you've seen in design magazines? That's it.

Verified architect. Verified celebrity owner. Verified press history.

Comment HOPE if you've ever toured it (or want the dossier).

#bobhope #johnlautner #palmsprings #mushroomhouse #midcenturymodern #realestate

Walt Disney lived in Palm Springs from 1948 to 1966. Here's where.

Smoke Tree Ranch. A 50-home gated equestrian community founded in 1936. Disney bought in 1948, summered there for 18 years, and the deed history is publicly verifiable.

There are still ~50 homes in the ranch. Each has its own pedigree.

Comment SMOKE for the full neighborhood dossier.

#waltdisney #palmsprings #smoketreeranch #disneyhistory #midcenturymodern #realestate

If you're looking at a Palm Springs MCM home — ask for the dossier first.

Permit history. Architect attribution. Celebrity ownership. Period press features. National Register status.

These aren't "nice to haves." They are 8–15% of the resale value.

We build them for $5M+ estates.

DM DOSSIER if you're listing or buying — we'll generate one free for your address.

#palmsprings #realestate #midcenturymodern #realestateagent #luxuryrealestate #proptech

Liberace lived here. The pool was shaped like a piano.

Casa de Liberace. 501 N Belardo. Owned 1968–1987. Verified through the Liberace Foundation archive + Riverside County deeds + Palm Springs Life feature 1973.

The piano pool was filled in in the 1990s. The dossier shows you the original.

Comment LIBERACE for the Casa de Liberace dossier.

#liberace #palmsprings #celebrityhomes #pianopool #realestate #vintagepalmsprings

What 16,787 pedigree-classified properties told us about the Coachella Valley.

53 verified A-tier estates (architect + celebrity).
1,282 B-tier (top neighborhood, MCM era).
5,161 C-tier.
10,317 D-tier.

13 named neighborhoods. 11 documented architects. Every claim cited to a primary source.

Comment INDEX for the full pedigree breakdown.

#palmsprings #realestate #midcenturymodern #realestatedata #proptech #luxuryrealestate

John Lautner designed 8 homes in Palm Springs. Originals trade once every 4.7 years.

That's not marketing — that's the actual median trade frequency from the Lautner Foundation registry.

When one comes on market, it doesn't sit. The Elrod House. The Hoover Residence. The Walstrom House. The Bob Hope House.

Comment LAUTNER for the full architect portfolio.

#johnlautner #palmsprings #midcenturymodern #architecture #lautner #desertmodernism

Want to buy a verified Albert Frey? You've got 47 options.

Frey designed 47 documented residential commissions in Palm Springs over a 50-year career. The Tramway Gas Station. Frey House I. Frey House II. The Loewy House. The Cree House.

Verified attribution = 5-12% premium at resale. Unverified = nothing.

Comment FREY47 for the full Frey commissions list.

#albertfrey #palmsprings #midcenturymodern #architecture #desertmodernism #realestate

Three things every buyer of a $5M+ Palm Springs home should ask for.

1. Architect drawings (UCSB, UCLA, or Lautner Foundation archive copy)
2. Permit history with dates + scope (Riverside County records)
3. Period press features (Architectural Digest, Palm Springs Life, Look Magazine)

If the listing agent can't produce these — there's no provenance file.

Comment DUE if you want the buyer's due diligence checklist.

#palmsprings #luxuryrealestate #duediligence #midcenturymodern #buyersagent #proptech

Every property has a story. Most agents never tell it.

We pulled the deed history on a Movie Colony estate. 11 owners since 1948. Three of them were Hollywood. One of them was a U.S. Senator. The current listing photo doesn't mention any of it.

The seller is leaving money on the table because the story isn't being told.

Comment STORY if you own one of these homes — we'll write its dossier.

#palmsprings #realestate #moviecolony #celebrityhomes #realestateagent #luxuryrealestate
